tasks.text: Commit 4 uncommitted hub files and stress-test approval gateway
tasks.description: Commit 4 uncommitted Hub files that were left in working tree during the standup system overhaul. Then stress-test the new ticket approval gateway by running a mock standup and verifying the full approval flow works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: All uncommitted Hub files are committed and pushed to main
Approval gateway successfully processes at least one mock standup
Approve and reject buttons both function correctly in Board Room

tasks.text: Document rollback procedure and establish monitoring dashboard
tasks.description: Create a rollback procedure document for the standup system in case of failures, and set up a monitoring dashboard to track standup health, ticket throughput, and approval rates over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Rollback procedure documented with step-by-step recovery instructions
Monitoring dashboard tracks standup run success/failure rate
Dashboard shows ticket approval/rejection metrics

tasks.text: Define and track shipping velocity success metrics
tasks.description: Define what 'shipping velocity' means for the org — commits per week, features shipped, tickets closed — and establish a tracking system to measure it consistently. This metric will drive accountability across all agents.
acceptanceCriteria: Shipping velocity metric clearly defined with specific measurable components
Baseline measurement established from last 30 days of activity
Tracking method set up (dashboard, cron report, or weekly summary)

tasks.text: Submit current Lever build to App Store as planned
tasks.description: Take the current Lever app build through The Catapult deployment pipeline and submit it to the App Store via TestFlight. This is the planned release after recent faith and family feature work.
acceptanceCriteria: Lever build passes all Catapult pre-flight checks
Build successfully uploaded to TestFlight
App available for testing in TestFlight within 24 hours

tasks.text: Draft technical specification for shared React Native architecture
tasks.description: Draft a technical specification for how the Lever app and a potential separated financial app would share React Native architecture — shared components, navigation patterns, data layer, and deployment pipeline using the Expo/Turborepo monorepo structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Technical spec covers shared component library design
Spec addresses data layer sharing (Supabase, state management)
Spec includes build/deploy pipeline for multiple app targets from monorepo
Document reviewed and approved by CEO

tasks.text: Design user behavior instrumentation for separation validation
tasks.description: Design an instrumentation plan to track user behavior across faith/family features vs. financial features in Lever. This data will validate whether app separation makes sense based on actual usage patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: Instrumentation plan identifies key user behavior events to track
Plan covers both faith/family and financial feature usage
Analytics approach can determine feature overlap vs. distinct user segments

tasks.text: Document financial feature migration boundaries
tasks.description: Document clear boundaries for which financial features belong in a separated financial app vs. which stay in Lever. Cover portfolio tracking, position management, trading signals, and financial health tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Every financial feature categorized as 'migrate' or 'keep in Lever'
Migration boundaries justified with user experience rationale
Data sharing requirements between apps clearly defined

tasks.text: Develop distinct brand positioning for dual apps
tasks.description: Develop distinct brand positioning for the Lever faith/family app and the potential separated financial app. Each needs its own identity, value proposition, and target audience definition while maintaining brand coherence.
acceptanceCriteria: Brand positioning document for each app with unique value props
Target audience defined for each app
Visual identity direction proposed (colors, tone, naming suggestions)

tasks.text: Generate daily devotional content based on 8 faith dimensions
tasks.description: Generate daily devotional content that maps to Lever's 8 faith dimensions (Prayer, Scripture, Gratitude, Service, Fellowship, Worship, Rest, Growth). Each devotional should be practical, actionable, and tied to the dimension's scoring criteria.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 7 devotionals created (one per day of the week)
Each devotional maps to one or more of the 8 faith dimensions
Content is practical and includes actionable reflection prompts
Formatted for display in Lever's faith tab UI

tasks.text: Draft faith lesson content for the Lever app (currently 7 lessons)
tasks.description: Draft new faith lesson content to expand beyond the current 7 lessons in Lever. Lessons should cover deeper topics across the 8 faith dimensions with progressive difficulty and practical application.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 new lesson outlines drafted
Lessons cover dimensions not well-represented in current 7
Each lesson includes learning objectives and practical exercises
Content formatted for Lever's lesson display component

tasks.text: Draft feature spec for expanding Family tab (currently 455 lines)

acceptanceCriteria: Feature spec covers family touchpoint tracker with frequency goals
Relationship health scoring algorithm proposed
Family member profile enhancements specified
Spec includes UI wireframes or component descriptions

tasks.text: Design prototype agent canvas experience - single screen with 2-widget dynamic display
tasks.description: Design a prototype of the agent canvas experience: a single blank screen where the user meets their personal AI agent. The agent asks 3 key questions about life priorities, then dynamically surfaces 2-3 relevant capability cards. This is the minimum viable agent-first experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Single-screen prototype with conversational agent onboarding
Agent asks 3 priority questions and generates personalized card layout
At least 3 capability cards can be dynamically surfaced
User can rearrange cards through conversation

tasks.text: Architect widget system migration for current Lever tabs
tasks.description: Design the technical architecture for migrating current Lever tabs (Faith, Family, Finance) into a composable widget/card system. Each existing tab becomes a widget block that can be arranged, hidden, or surfaced by the agent. Must work within current Expo/React Native/Turborepo stack.
acceptanceCriteria: Widget registry architecture documented with API contracts
Migration path from current tab-based UI to widget system defined
Backward compatibility plan for existing Lever users

tasks.text: Develop agent onboarding psychological framework and guardrails
tasks.description: Develop the psychological framework for how the personal agent onboards users — what questions to ask, how to build trust progressively, ethical guardrails for an AI that learns deeply about someone's life. Ensure the agent genuinely helps rather than manipulates.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding question framework with progressive depth levels
Ethical guardrails document for agent-user relationships
Trust-building progression model (day 1 → week 1 → month 1)

tasks.text: Define revenue model for agent-canvas hybrid architecture
tasks.description: Define the revenue model for an agent-canvas hybrid app. Explore: free agent + premium capability blocks, subscription tiers, freemium with advanced agent features, marketplace for third-party widgets. Must be sustainable without exploiting the agent relationship.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 3 revenue model options analyzed with projections
Pricing tiers defined with feature boundaries
Unit economics estimated (CAC, LTV, margin per tier)

tasks.text: Create marketing narrative for agent-first personal app category
tasks.description: Create the marketing narrative for a new app category: the agent-first personal OS. Position against both traditional apps (static, one-size-fits-all) and horizontal AI assistants (ChatGPT, Apple Intelligence). Our advantage: vertical depth in faith, family, and finance with a personal relationship layer.
acceptanceCriteria: Category-defining positioning statement drafted
Competitive landscape analysis vs. horizontal AI + vertical apps
Launch narrative and tagline options presented

tasks.resolution: Audited: root package-lock.json committed and tracked. 30 floating deps (normal for Expo — ^/~ ranges pinned by lockfile). npm workspaces monorepo structure correct. Build 5 failure was transient network/registry issue, not dep management. No code changes needed — lockfile provides deterministic installs.
